proven.claim: PROVEN: available at line 11 equals the return value of db.getStock (assignment identity)
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; PROVEN: available at line 11 equals the return value of db.getStock (assignment identity)
; Proof by contradiction: precondition (transition) + negation → unsat
(declare-const getStock_return Int)
(declare-const available Int)
; Transition (line 10): available = db.getStock(productId)
(assert (= available getStock_return))
; Negation of property: available ≠ getStock_return
(assert (not (= available getStock_return)))
(check-sat)
; Expected: unsat — the assignment is an identity; the logged value is exactly what getStock returned

violations.claim: VIOLATION: db.getStock has no lower-bound contract; available can be negative
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P3
; VIOLATION: db.getStock has no lower-bound contract; available can be negative
; The code assigns available = db.getStock() with no post-call validation.
; A negative value is semantically invalid stock and would be silently logged/returned.
(declare-const getStock_return Int)
(declare-const available Int)
; Transition (line 10): available = db.getStock(productId)
(assert (= available getStock_return))
; db.getStock signature is (): number — no contract establishes a lower bound of 0
; No guard exists between line 10 and line 11
; Violation: negative available reaches the log and the return
(assert (< available 0))
(check-sat)
; Expected: sat — negative stock is reachable; no validation prevents it
; Concrete witness: getStock_return = -1 → available = -1

violations.claim: VIOLATION: reserveStock mutates setAvailable but never setStock.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P2
; VIOLATION: reserveStock mutates setAvailable but never setStock.
; After any reservation, getStock is permanently stale.
; checkAvailability will log/return the original pre-reservation total indefinitely.
(declare-const initial_stock Int)
(declare-const initial_available Int)
(declare-const quantity Int)
(declare-const available_after_reserve Int)
; Setup: initially consistent state
(assert (> initial_stock 0))
(assert (= initial_available initial_stock))
(assert (> quantity 0))
(assert (<= quantity initial_available))
; Transition: reserveStock calls setAvailable (line 21 of reserveStock)
;   db.setAvailable(productId, available - quantity)
; db.setStock is NEVER called by any function in this module
(assert (= available_after_reserve (- initial_available quantity)))
; After reservation: checkAvailability still reads getStock = initial_stock (unchanged)
; Violation: logged/returned value does not reflect post-reservation availability
(assert (not (= initial_stock available_after_reserve)))
(check-sat)
; Expected: sat — checkAvailability returns the stale total-stock figure, not true available
; Witness: initial_stock=10, quantity=3 → checkAvailability logs 10, true available is 7

violations.claim: VIOLATION: releaseStock mutates setAvailable (adds back) but never setStock.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P2
; VIOLATION: releaseStock mutates setAvailable (adds back) but never setStock.
; After a release, getStock still reflects the original count; getAvailable is higher.
; checkAvailability can then log a value LESS than actual available units.
(declare-const original_stock Int)
(declare-const available_before_release Int)
(declare-const reserved_qty Int)
(declare-const available_after_release Int)
; State prior to release: available < stock (due to earlier reservations)
(assert (> original_stock 0))
(assert (> reserved_qty 0))
(assert (< available_before_release original_stock))
(assert (= available_before_release (- original_stock reserved_qty)))
; Transition: releaseStock calls setAvailable(productId, available + quantity)
(assert (= available_after_release (+ available_before_release reserved_qty)))
; db.setStock is NEVER called — getStock still returns original_stock
; Violation: checkAvailability reports original_stock, but true available = available_after_release
; These are equal only if no reservations ever happened — generally false
(assert (= available_after_release original_stock))   ; they coincide only at trivial base case
(assert (> reserved_qty 0))                           ; non-trivial: something was reserved
(check-sat)
; Expected: sat — demonstrates the only non-divergence case is when reserved_qty=0,
; meaning any real reservation/release cycle leaves getStock permanently inconsistent
; Witness: original_stock=10, reserved_qty=3 → release returns available_after_release=10=original_stock
; but only by accident of full release; partial release leaves them permanently diverged

violations.claim: VIOLATION: available = 0 is reachable; checkAvailability returns 0 with no error signal.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P6
; VIOLATION: available = 0 is reachable; checkAvailability returns 0 with no error signal.
; A caller cannot distinguish "no stock data" from "zero stock available."
; The function still returns normally, masking upstream errors (empty DB, failed load).
(declare-const getStock_return Int)
(declare-const available Int)
; Transition (line 10): available = db.getStock(productId)
(assert (= available getStock_return))
; Boundary: getStock returns 0 — stub implementation returns exactly 0
(assert (= getStock_return 0))
; Function logs and returns 0 without throwing, without signaling error
(assert (= available 0))
(check-sat)
; Expected: sat — zero-stock is logged and returned as a valid result;
; no sentinel distinguishes "product has 0 units" from "product not found / DB error"

proven.claim: PROVEN: At line 18, `available` and `reserved` are exactly the return
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; PROVEN: At line 18, `available` and `reserved` are exactly the return
; values of db.getAvailable and db.getReserved (assignment identity).
; Negation: the logged values could differ from db return values → expect unsat.
(declare-const available Int)
(declare-const reserved Int)
(declare-const getAvailable_result Int)
(declare-const getReserved_result Int)
; Transitions: lines 16-17 bind via direct assignment
(assert (= available getAvailable_result))
(assert (= reserved getReserved_result))
; Negation of property
(assert (or
  (not (= available getAvailable_result))
  (not (= reserved getReserved_result))))
(check-sat)
; unsat → the values logged at line 18 are exactly what db returned

violations.claim: VIOLATION: available - quantity underflows (oversell).
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P7
; VIOLATION: available - quantity underflows (oversell).
; reserveStock performs no guard on (quantity <= available) before line 18.
; The subsequent mutation at line 20 writes a negative availability.
(declare-const available Int)
(declare-const quantity Int)
(declare-const new_available Int)
; Transition line 16: available = db.getAvailable — no lower-bound contract
(assert (>= available 0))
; Transition line 20: new_available = available - quantity (mutation after log)
(assert (= new_available (- available quantity)))
; No quantity <= available guard exists anywhere before line 18
; Violation: new_available is negative
(assert (> quantity 0))
(assert (< new_available 0))
(check-sat)
; sat → e.g., available=3, quantity=10, new_available=-7
; db.setAvailable stores a negative stock value; the log at line 18 is the
; last observable state before this silent corruption occurs.

violations.claim: VIOLATION: quantity is caller-supplied with no positivity check before line 18.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P3
; VIOLATION: quantity is caller-supplied with no positivity check before line 18.
; A zero-quantity reservation is logged as successful, then executes as a no-op.
(declare-const available Int)
(declare-const reserved Int)
(declare-const quantity Int)
(declare-const new_available Int)
(declare-const new_reserved Int)
; Transitions lines 16-17: bind from db (no constraints imposed)
(assert (>= available 0))
(assert (>= reserved 0))
; quantity arrives at line 18 with no validation
(assert (= quantity 0))
; Transitions lines 20-21: mutations execute
(assert (= new_available (- available quantity)))
(assert (= new_reserved (+ reserved quantity)))
; Violation: mutations are no-ops despite a "successful" reservation being logged
(assert (= new_available available))
(assert (= new_reserved reserved))
(check-sat)
; sat → quantity=0 logs a reservation and calls setAvailable/setReserved
; but neither db field changes. The reservation silently succeeds as a no-op.

violations.claim: VIOLATION: negative quantity inverts reserveStock semantics.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P3
; VIOLATION: negative quantity inverts reserveStock semantics.
; quantity < 0 increases available and decreases reserved —
; the opposite of a reservation — with no guard before line 18.
(declare-const quantity Int)
(declare-const available Int)
(declare-const reserved Int)
(declare-const new_available Int)
(declare-const new_reserved Int)
; Transitions lines 16-17
(assert (>= available 0))
(assert (> reserved 0))
; Caller passes a negative quantity; no guard stops it
(assert (< quantity 0))
; Transitions lines 20-21
(assert (= new_available (- available quantity)))  ; available - neg = available + |q|
(assert (= new_reserved (+ reserved quantity)))    ; reserved + neg = reserved - |q|
; Violation: reserved goes negative
(assert (< new_reserved 0))
(check-sat)
; sat → e.g., reserved=2, quantity=-5 → new_reserved=-3
; A "reservation" releases stock and destroys the reserved counter.

violations.claim: VIOLATION: Two calls to reserveStock on the same productId.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P4
; VIOLATION: Two calls to reserveStock on the same productId.
; The first call mutates db.getAvailable; the second call reads the mutated value.
; Even if the first call was individually valid, the second causes oversell.
(declare-const available_init Int)
(declare-const quantity_1 Int)
(declare-const quantity_2 Int)
(declare-const available_after_1 Int)
(declare-const new_available_2 Int)
; First call: assume individually valid preconditions hold
(assert (> quantity_1 0))
(assert (>= available_init quantity_1))
; Transition: first call mutates db.setAvailable (line 20)
(assert (= available_after_1 (- available_init quantity_1)))
; Second call: reads the already-reduced state, no re-check
(assert (> quantity_2 0))
; Transition: second call's new_available computation
(assert (= new_available_2 (- available_after_1 quantity_2)))
; Violation: second call drives availability negative
(assert (< new_available_2 0))
(check-sat)
; sat → e.g., available=5, q1=3, available_after=2, q2=3, new=-1
; No idempotency guard; concurrent or sequential double-calls oversell silently.

violations.claim: VIOLATION: checkAvailability reads db.getStock; reserveStock reads db.getAvailable.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P2
; VIOLATION: checkAvailability reads db.getStock; reserveStock reads db.getAvailable.
; These are separate unlinked fields. A caller who verifies stock via
; checkAvailability and then calls reserveStock operates on divergent state.
(declare-const stock Int)        ; db.getStock — what checkAvailability returns
(declare-const available Int)    ; db.getAvailable — what reserveStock reads at line 16
(declare-const quantity Int)
(declare-const new_available Int)
; Caller checked stock as sufficient
(assert (> quantity 0))
(assert (>= stock quantity))
; Transition line 16: reserveStock reads a DIFFERENT field — available can be less
(assert (= new_available (- available quantity)))
; available and stock are not constrained to be equal (separate db fields)
(assert (< available quantity))
; Violation: new_available is negative despite stock check passing
(assert (< new_available 0))
(check-sat)
; sat → stock=10, quantity=5, available=2: caller's stock check passes,
; but reserveStock uses the getAvailable field (=2), causing oversell.
; The two db fields silently diverge; no synchronization exists.

violations.claim: VIOLATION: available = 0 at line 18 is reachable (no lower-bound contract
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P6
; VIOLATION: available = 0 at line 18 is reachable (no lower-bound contract
; on db.getAvailable). A positive-quantity reservation against zero stock
; proceeds past the log without an error signal.
(declare-const available Int)
(declare-const quantity Int)
(declare-const new_available Int)
; Transition line 16: available = db.getAvailable — zero stock is a valid return
(assert (= available 0))
; Caller passes a positive quantity (well-formed request)
(assert (> quantity 0))
; Transition line 20: new_available = available - quantity
(assert (= new_available (- available quantity)))
; Violation: result is negative; the log at line 18 is the last signal before write
(assert (< new_available 0))
(check-sat)
; sat → available=0, quantity=1 → new_available=-1
; The console.log at line 18 faithfully prints "0 available" but no error is raised;
; db.setAvailable writes -1 on the very next line.

proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; PROVE: newAvailable + newReserved = available + reserved
; Method: assert transitions + negated property → expect unsat
(declare-const available Int)
(declare-const reserved Int)
(declare-const quantity Int)
(declare-const newAvailable Int)
(declare-const newReserved Int)
; Transition (line 24): newAvailable = available - quantity
(assert (= newAvailable (- available quantity)))
; Transition (line 25): newReserved = reserved + quantity
(assert (= newReserved (+ reserved quantity)))
; Negation of conservation property
(assert (not (= (+ newAvailable newReserved) (+ available reserved))))
(check-sat)
; Expected: unsat — conservation is algebraically guaranteed by the transitions

; PRINCIPLE: P5
; PROVE: newAvailable (logged at line 25) = db_available_written (line 21)
(declare-const available Int)
(declare-const quantity Int)
(declare-const db_available_written Int)
(declare-const newAvailable Int)
; Transition (line 21): db.setAvailable writes available - quantity
(assert (= db_available_written (- available quantity)))
; Transition (line 24): newAvailable = available - quantity
(assert (= newAvailable (- available quantity)))
; Negation: log disagrees with DB write
(assert (not (= newAvailable db_available_written)))
(check-sat)
; Expected: unsat — both expressions are (available - quantity); they are identical

; PRINCIPLE: P5
; PROVE: newReserved (logged at line 25) = db_reserved_written (line 22)
(declare-const reserved Int)
(declare-const quantity Int)
(declare-const db_reserved_written Int)
(declare-const newReserved Int)
; Transition (line 22): db.setReserved writes reserved + quantity
(assert (= db_reserved_written (+ reserved quantity)))
; Transition (line 25): newReserved = reserved + quantity
(assert (= newReserved (+ reserved quantity)))
; Negation: log disagrees with DB write
(assert (not (= newReserved db_reserved_written)))
(check-sat)
; Expected: unsat — both are (reserved + quantity); identically computed

violations.claim: VIOLATION: negative available stock committed and logged as success
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P7
; VIOLATION: negative available stock committed and logged as success
(declare-const available Int)
(declare-const quantity Int)
(declare-const newAvailable Int)
; Code establishes: available >= 0 (reasonable DB invariant, no enforcement)
(assert (>= available 0))
; Code establishes: quantity > 0 (caller-supplied, no positivity check in reserveStock)
(assert (> quantity 0))
; No check quantity <= available exists anywhere in reserveStock
; Oversell condition: caller requests more than available
(assert (> quantity available))
; Transition (line 24): newAvailable = available - quantity
(assert (= newAvailable (- available quantity)))
; Violation: negative available stock is logged as "Reservation complete"
(assert (< newAvailable 0))
(check-sat)
; Expected: sat — e.g. available=3, quantity=5 → newAvailable=-2, logged as success
; The DB write at line 21 already committed this negative value before line 25 fires

violations.claim: VIOLATION: negative quantity makes newReserved decrease; log still says "complete"
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P3
; VIOLATION: negative quantity makes newReserved decrease; log still says "complete"
(declare-const reserved Int)
(declare-const quantity Int)
(declare-const newReserved Int)
; Code establishes: reserved >= 0 (reasonable DB invariant)
(assert (>= reserved 0))
; quantity is caller-supplied — no sign check in reserveStock
(assert (< quantity 0))
; Transition (line 25): newReserved = reserved + quantity
(assert (= newReserved (+ reserved quantity)))
; Violation: newReserved < reserved — reservation decreased the reserved count
(assert (< newReserved reserved))
(check-sat)
; Expected: sat — e.g. quantity=-3, reserved=5 → newReserved=2
; The log announces "Reservation complete: X available, 2 reserved" masking the un-reserve

violations.claim: VIOLATION: newReserved < 0 is committed and logged as "Reservation complete"
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P7
; VIOLATION: newReserved < 0 is committed and logged as "Reservation complete"
(declare-const reserved Int)
(declare-const quantity Int)
(declare-const newReserved Int)
; Code establishes: reserved >= 0 (no lower-bound contract on db.getReserved enforced)
(assert (>= reserved 0))
; quantity is caller-supplied and negative; |quantity| > reserved
(assert (< quantity 0))
(assert (> (- quantity) reserved))  ; i.e., -quantity > reserved
; Transition (line 25): newReserved = reserved + quantity
(assert (= newReserved (+ reserved quantity)))
; Violation: reserved count is now physically impossible (negative)
(assert (< newReserved 0))
(check-sat)
; Expected: sat — e.g. quantity=-5, reserved=2 → newReserved=-3, committed + logged

violations.claim: [NEW] PRINCIPLE: Post-mutation logging — line 25 fires after DB writes are committed
violations.smt2:
; [NEW] PRINCIPLE: Post-mutation logging — line 25 fires after DB writes are committed
; The log provides no conditional branch; it cannot signal failure.
; Caller cannot distinguish a valid reservation from an oversell.
(declare-const available Int)
(declare-const quantity Int)
(declare-const db_available_written Int)   ; committed at line 21
(declare-const db_reserved_written Int)    ; committed at line 22
(declare-const newAvailable Int)           ; logged at line 25
(declare-const newReserved Int)            ; logged at line 25
; Transition (line 21): DB write already executed
(assert (= db_available_written (- available quantity)))
; Transition (line 22): DB write already executed
(assert (= db_reserved_written (+ db_available_written quantity)))
; Transition (lines 24-25): log variables mirror the DB writes
(assert (= newAvailable db_available_written))
; Oversell: available < quantity, no validation guard exists in reserveStock
(assert (>= available 0))
(assert (> quantity available))
; Both DB writes are committed AND line 25 is reached AND newAvailable < 0
(assert (< db_available_written 0))
(assert (< newAvailable 0))
(check-sat)
; Expected: sat — the corruption is already in the DB when the log fires;
; the function returns void; the log always prints "Reservation complete";
; no exception, no return code, no conditional message distinguishes this from success.
; The caller has no recourse once reserveStock returns.

clause_history.clause:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; PROVE: newAvailable + newReserved = available + reserved
; Method: assert transitions + negated property → expect unsat
(declare-const available Int)
(declare-const reserved Int)
(declare-const quantity Int)
(declare-const newAvailable Int)
(declare-const newReserved Int)
; Transition (line 24): newAvailable = available - quantity
(assert (= newAvailable (- available quantity)))
; Transition (line 25): newReserved = reserved + quantity
(assert (= newReserved (+ reserved quantity)))
; Negation of conservation property
(assert (not (= (+ newAvailable newReserved) (+ available reserved))))
(check-sat)
; Expected: unsat — conservation is algebraically guaranteed by the transitions

; PRINCIPLE: P5
; PROVE: newAvailable (logged at line 25) = db_available_written (line 21)
(declare-const available Int)
(declare-const quantity Int)
(declare-const db_available_written Int)
(declare-const newAvailable Int)
; Transition (line 21): db.setAvailable writes available - quantity
(assert (= db_available_written (- available quantity)))
; Transition (line 24): newAvailable = available - quantity
(assert (= newAvailable (- available quantity)))
; Negation: log disagrees with DB write
(assert (not (= newAvailable db_available_written)))
(check-sat)
; Expected: unsat — both expressions are (available - quantity); they are identical

; PRINCIPLE: P5
; PROVE: newReserved (logged at line 25) = db_reserved_written (line 22)
(declare-const reserved Int)
(declare-const quantity Int)
(declare-const db_reserved_written Int)
(declare-const newReserved Int)
; Transition (line 22): db.setReserved writes reserved + quantity
(assert (= db_reserved_written (+ reserved quantity)))
; Transition (line 25): newReserved = reserved + quantity
(assert (= newReserved (+ reserved quantity)))
; Negation: log disagrees with DB write
(assert (not (= newReserved db_reserved_written)))
(check-sat)
; Expected: unsat — both are (reserved + quantity); identically computed

; PRINCIPLE: P7
; VIOLATION: negative available stock committed and logged as success
(declare-const available Int)
(declare-const quantity Int)
(declare-const newAvailable Int)
; Code establishes: available >= 0 (reasonable DB invariant, no enforcement)
(assert (>= available 0))
; Code establishes: quantity > 0 (caller-supplied, no positivity check in reserveStock)
(assert (> quantity 0))
; No check quantity <= available exists anywhere in reserveStock
; Oversell condition: caller requests more than available
(assert (> quantity available))
; Transition (line 24): newAvailable = available - quantity
(assert (= newAvailable (- available quantity)))
; Violation: negative available stock is logged as "Reservation complete"
(assert (< newAvailable 0))
(check-sat)
; Expected: sat — e.g. available=3, quantity=5 → newAvailable=-2, logged as success
; The DB write at line 21 already committed this negative value before line 25 fires

; PRINCIPLE: P3
; VIOLATION: negative quantity makes newReserved decrease; log still says "complete"
(declare-const reserved Int)
(declare-const quantity Int)
(declare-const newReserved Int)
; Code establishes: reserved >= 0 (reasonable DB invariant)
(assert (>= reserved 0))
; quantity is caller-supplied — no sign check in reserveStock
(assert (< quantity 0))
; Transition (line 25): newReserved = reserved + quantity
(assert (= newReserved (+ reserved quantity)))
; Violation: newReserved < reserved — reservation decreased the reserved count
(assert (< newReserved reserved))
(check-sat)
; Expected: sat — e.g. quantity=-3, reserved=5 → newReserved=2
; The log announces "Reservation complete: X available, 2 reserved" masking the un-reserve

; PRINCIPLE: P7

(declare-const reserved Int)
(declare-const quantity Int)
(declare-const newReserved Int)
; Code establishes: reserved >= 0 (no lower-bound contract on db.getReserved enforced)
(assert (>= reserved 0))
; quantity is caller-supplied and negative; |quantity| > reserved
(assert (< quantity 0))

; Transition (line 25): newReserved = reserved + quantity
(assert (= newReserved (+ reserved quantity)))
; Violation: reserved count is now physically impossible (negative)
(assert (< newReserved 0))
(check-sat)

; The log provides no conditional branch; it cannot signal failure.

(declare-const available Int)

(declare-const newReserved Int)            ; logged at line 25

; Transition (line 22): DB write already executed

proven.claim: PROVEN: At line 31, `available` and `reserved` are exact identity bindings
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; PROVEN: At line 31, `available` and `reserved` are exact identity bindings
; from db.getAvailable (line 29) and db.getReserved (line 30).
; No transformation occurs between those reads and the console.log at line 31.
(declare-const available Int)
(declare-const db_available_out Int)
(declare-const reserved Int)
(declare-const db_reserved_out Int)
; Transitions: direct assignments at lines 29-30
(assert (= available db_available_out))
(assert (= reserved db_reserved_out))
; Negate: at least one binding is broken
(assert (or
  (not (= available db_available_out))
  (not (= reserved db_reserved_out))))
(check-sat)
; expect: unsat — both are identity assignments with no intervening transformation

violations.claim: VIOLATION: At line 31, no guard ensures quantity <= reserved.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P7
; VIOLATION: At line 31, no guard ensures quantity <= reserved.
; db.setReserved(productId, reserved - quantity) at line 34 will commit a negative value.
(declare-const reserved Int)
(declare-const quantity Int)
(declare-const new_reserved Int)
; Transition: line 34 computes reserved - quantity and writes it
(assert (= new_reserved (- reserved quantity)))
; Code constraint: reserved comes from db.getReserved — no lower bound enforced
; Optimistic: assume prior reserveStock left reserved >= 0
(assert (>= reserved 0))
; Code constraint: quantity is caller-supplied; releaseStock validates nothing
(assert (> quantity 0))
; Violation: quantity exceeds reserved — negative value committed after line 31 logs success
(assert (> quantity reserved))
(assert (< new_reserved 0))
(check-sat)
; expect: sat — e.g. reserved=2, quantity=5, new_reserved=-3
; The log at line 31 announces the release; line 34 commits the invalid state.

violations.claim: VIOLATION: quantity is caller-supplied with no positivity check before line 31.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P3
; VIOLATION: quantity is caller-supplied with no positivity check before line 31.
; A negative quantity inverts releaseStock semantics at lines 33-34:
; available DECREASES and reserved INCREASES — opposite of the function's intent.
(declare-const available Int)
(declare-const reserved Int)
(declare-const quantity Int)
(declare-const new_available Int)
(declare-const new_reserved Int)
; Transitions: lines 33-34 always execute unconditionally
(assert (= new_available (+ available quantity)))
(assert (= new_reserved (- reserved quantity)))
; releaseStock validates nothing — quantity sign is unchecked at line 31
(assert (< quantity 0))
; DB reads: assume valid prior state
(assert (>= available 0))
(assert (>= reserved 0))
; Violation: semantics inverted — available shrinks, reserved grows
(assert (< new_available available))
(assert (> new_reserved reserved))
(check-sat)
; expect: sat — e.g. quantity=-3, available=10→7, reserved=2→5

violations.claim: VIOLATION: releaseStock does not establish the precondition
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P1
; VIOLATION: releaseStock does not establish the precondition
; available + quantity >= 0 before calling db.setAvailable at line 33.
; If available is negative (left by a prior oversell in reserveStock),
; the release can push it further negative.
(declare-const available Int)
(declare-const quantity Int)
(declare-const new_available Int)
; Transition: line 33 computes and commits available + quantity
(assert (= new_available (+ available quantity)))
; Code constraint: available comes from db.getAvailable after a prior oversell
; Existing contract [P7, reserveStock line 18] proves available can be negative
(assert (< available 0))
; Code constraint: quantity is positive — a valid-looking release
(assert (> quantity 0))
; Code constraint: quantity is not large enough to cancel the negative available
(assert (< quantity (- available)))  ; |quantity| < |available|
; Violation: db.setAvailable commits a negative value — precondition not established
(assert (< new_available 0))
(check-sat)
; expect: sat — e.g. available=-10, quantity=3, new_available=-7

violations.claim: VIOLATION: Two calls to releaseStock on the same productId.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P4
; VIOLATION: Two calls to releaseStock on the same productId.
; The second call's reserved starts from the value the first call wrote.
; Even with individually valid-looking quantities, the combined release overshoots.
(declare-const reserved_initial Int)
(declare-const quantity_1 Int)
(declare-const quantity_2 Int)
(declare-const reserved_after_1 Int)
(declare-const reserved_after_2 Int)
; Transition: first call — line 34 writes reserved - quantity_1
(assert (= reserved_after_1 (- reserved_initial quantity_1)))
; Transition: second call reads reserved_after_1 as its "reserved", then writes
(assert (= reserved_after_2 (- reserved_after_1 quantity_2)))
; Both calls receive positive quantities
(assert (> quantity_1 0))
(assert (> quantity_2 0))
; First call looks valid: reserved_initial >= quantity_1
(assert (>= reserved_initial quantity_1))
; Second call drives reserved negative
(assert (> quantity_2 reserved_after_1))
(assert (< reserved_after_2 0))
(check-sat)
; expect: sat — e.g. reserved_initial=5, q1=4, reserved_after_1=1, q2=3, reserved_after_2=-2

violations.claim: VIOLATION: quantity = 0 is a degenerate input that reaches line 31 unchecked.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P6
; VIOLATION: quantity = 0 is a degenerate input that reaches line 31 unchecked.
; The function logs "Releasing 0 of X back to stock" then performs a no-op write pair,
; committing available+0 and reserved-0. No guard exists before line 31.
(declare-const quantity Int)
(declare-const available Int)
(declare-const reserved Int)
(declare-const new_available Int)
(declare-const new_reserved Int)
; Transitions: lines 33-34 execute unconditionally
(assert (= new_available (+ available quantity)))
(assert (= new_reserved (- reserved quantity)))
; Degenerate input: zero quantity, no validation in releaseStock
(assert (= quantity 0))
(assert (>= available 0))
(assert (>= reserved 0))
; Consequence: mutations are pure no-ops — identical to pre-call state
(assert (= new_available available))
(assert (= new_reserved reserved))
(check-sat)
; expect: sat — zero-quantity release passes silently through line 31, logs and writes anyway

violations.claim: [NEW] PRINCIPLE: Pre-mutation logging
violations.smt2:
; [NEW] PRINCIPLE: Pre-mutation logging
; VIOLATION: Line 31 fires BEFORE db.setAvailable (line 33) and db.setReserved (line 34).
; The log announces "Releasing quantity back to stock" without having validated that
; reserved >= quantity. A semantically invalid mutation is guaranteed to follow,
; but the log presents it as a pending success.
(declare-const quantity Int)
(declare-const reserved Int)
(declare-const new_reserved Int)
; Transition: log fires at line 31 — quantity is embedded in the message
; Transition: line 34 then commits reserved - quantity regardless
(assert (= new_reserved (- reserved quantity)))
; Code constraints: positive quantity, non-negative reserved (prior valid state)
(assert (> quantity 0))
(assert (>= reserved 0))
; Violation: log has already fired announcing the release,
; but the committed value is invalid — created a false audit record
(assert (> quantity reserved))
(assert (< new_reserved 0))
(check-sat)
; expect: sat — line 31 log fires unconditionally before the validation gap is exposed at line 34
; e.g. reserved=1, quantity=4: log says "Releasing 4", line 34 writes reserved=-3

proven.claim: PROVE: logged values at line 36 == DB-written values at lines 33-34
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; PROVE: logged values at line 36 == DB-written values at lines 33-34
; Method: assert both transitions, negate equality → expect unsat
(declare-const available Int)
(declare-const reserved Int)
(declare-const quantity Int)
(declare-const db_available_written Int)
(declare-const db_reserved_written Int)
(declare-const logged_available Int)
(declare-const logged_reserved Int)
; Transition: line 33 — db.setAvailable commits (available + quantity)
(assert (= db_available_written (+ available quantity)))
; Transition: line 34 — db.setReserved commits (reserved - quantity)
(assert (= db_reserved_written (- reserved quantity)))
; Transition: line 36 — log computes from same original bindings
(assert (= logged_available (+ available quantity)))
(assert (= logged_reserved (- reserved quantity)))
; Negate: can the log diverge from what was written?
(assert (or
  (not (= logged_available db_available_written))
  (not (= logged_reserved db_reserved_written))
))
(check-sat)
; unsat — the logged values are provably identical to the DB-written values.
; The log is an accurate mirror of the committed state (even if that state is invalid).

violations.claim: VIOLATION: reserved - quantity committed as negative, then logged as "Release complete"
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P7
; VIOLATION: reserved - quantity committed as negative, then logged as "Release complete"
; Model: reserved comes from DB (>= 0), quantity is caller-supplied (> 0, unchecked upper bound)
(declare-const available Int)
(declare-const reserved Int)
(declare-const quantity Int)
(declare-const db_reserved_written Int)
(declare-const logged_reserved Int)
; DB read at L30: reserved is non-negative (DB invariant, no contract violated yet)
(assert (>= reserved 0))
; L29: available is non-negative
(assert (>= available 0))
; quantity is caller-supplied — releaseStock performs no check: quantity <= reserved
(assert (> quantity 0))
; Transition L34: db.setReserved commits reserved - quantity
(assert (= db_reserved_written (- reserved quantity)))
; Transition L36: log reads same arithmetic — mirrors committed value
(assert (= logged_reserved (- reserved quantity)))
; No guard between L30 and L34 enforces quantity <= reserved
; Violation reachable: quantity > reserved
(assert (> quantity reserved))
; Consequence: negative value is committed AND logged as "Release complete"
(assert (< db_reserved_written 0))
(assert (= logged_reserved db_reserved_written))
(check-sat)
; sat — negative reserved stock is committed at L34 and confirmed at L36 as "complete"
; Witness: reserved=2, quantity=5 → db_reserved_written=-3, logged_reserved=-3

violations.claim: VIOLATION: negative quantity makes available decrease and reserved increase at line 36
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P3
; VIOLATION: negative quantity makes available decrease and reserved increase at line 36
; The log still announces "Release complete" despite inverse semantics
(declare-const available Int)
(declare-const reserved Int)
(declare-const quantity Int)
(declare-const logged_available Int)
(declare-const logged_reserved Int)
; DB read state: non-negative
(assert (>= available 0))
(assert (>= reserved 0))
; Transition L33: db.setAvailable(available + quantity) — quantity unchecked
(assert (= logged_available (+ available quantity)))
; Transition L34: db.setReserved(reserved - quantity)
(assert (= logged_reserved (- reserved quantity)))
; Caller supplies negative quantity — no guard in releaseStock prevents this
(assert (< quantity 0))
; Consequence: available DECREASES (opposite of release)
(assert (< logged_available available))
; Consequence: reserved INCREASES (opposite of release)
(assert (> logged_reserved reserved))
(check-sat)
; sat — negative quantity causes releaseStock to behave as reserveStock
; DB writes and log at L36 both confirm semantically inverted state as "Release complete"
; Witness: quantity=-3, available=5→2, reserved=4→7

violations.claim: VIOLATION: two releaseStock calls on the same productId
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P4
; VIOLATION: two releaseStock calls on the same productId
; First call mutates reserved; second call reads the reduced value and may overdraw
(declare-const reserved_0 Int)
(declare-const available_0 Int)
(declare-const quantity_1 Int)
(declare-const quantity_2 Int)
(declare-const reserved_after_call1 Int)
(declare-const available_after_call1 Int)
(declare-const logged_reserved_call2 Int)
; Initial DB state
(assert (>= reserved_0 0))
(assert (>= available_0 0))
; Both calls use positive quantities
(assert (> quantity_1 0))
(assert (> quantity_2 0))
; First call: transitions at L33-L34 mutate shared DB state
(assert (= reserved_after_call1 (- reserved_0 quantity_1)))
(assert (= available_after_call1 (+ available_0 quantity_1)))
; First call succeeds (quantity_1 <= reserved_0)
(assert (<= quantity_1 reserved_0))
; Second call L30 reads the mutated reserved state
; Second call L36: logs reserved_after_call1 - quantity_2
(assert (= logged_reserved_call2 (- reserved_after_call1 quantity_2)))
; Violation: second call overdraws what remains
(assert (> quantity_2 reserved_after_call1))
(assert (< logged_reserved_call2 0))
(check-sat)
; sat — two sequential releases on the same product drive reserved negative
; Witness: reserved_0=5, q1=3, reserved_after=2, q2=3 → logged=-1

violations.claim: VIOLATION: quantity = 0 passes all code paths and reaches line 36 unchallenged
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P6
; VIOLATION: quantity = 0 passes all code paths and reaches line 36 unchallenged
; DB state is unchanged; log still announces "Release complete"
(declare-const available Int)
(declare-const reserved Int)
(declare-const quantity Int)
(declare-const logged_available Int)
(declare-const logged_reserved Int)
; DB read state
(assert (>= available 0))
(assert (>= reserved 0))
; Transition L33-L34: writes happen regardless of quantity value
(assert (= logged_available (+ available quantity)))
(assert (= logged_reserved (- reserved quantity)))
; Degenerate: zero quantity — no guard exists before L33
(assert (= quantity 0))
; Consequence: DB and log show identical pre/post state — a committed no-op
(assert (= logged_available available))
(assert (= logged_reserved reserved))
(check-sat)
; sat — a zero-quantity release is committed and logged as "Release complete" with zero effect
; This masks upstream logic errors (wrong productId, wrong quantity binding, etc.)

violations.claim: [NEW] PRINCIPLE: Post-mutation logging
violations.smt2:
; [NEW] PRINCIPLE: Post-mutation logging
; VIOLATION: the log at L36 fires unconditionally after both DB writes are committed
; There is no conditional, no rollback, no exception path between L34 and L36
; A successful log emission is therefore NOT evidence of a valid release
(declare-const reserved Int)
(declare-const quantity Int)
(declare-const db_reserved_written Int)
(declare-const log_fires Int)         ; 1 = log fires, 0 = log suppressed
(declare-const logged_reserved Int)
; DB state: reserved is non-negative
(assert (>= reserved 0))
(assert (> quantity 0))
; Transition L34: DB write committed unconditionally
(assert (= db_reserved_written (- reserved quantity)))
; Structural fact: no branch between L34 and L36
; log_fires is always 1 — the log cannot be suppressed by invalid state
(assert (= log_fires 1))
(assert (= logged_reserved db_reserved_written))
; Violation: invalid state (negative reserved) committed
(assert (> quantity reserved))
(assert (< db_reserved_written 0))
; AND the log fires anyway, announcing success
(assert (= log_fires 1))
(check-sat)
; sat — L36 fires unconditionally after L34, even when db_reserved_written < 0
; "Release complete" is a structural lie: it proves execution reached L36, not correctness

clause_history.clause:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; PROVE: logged values at line 36 == DB-written values at lines 33-34
; Method: assert both transitions, negate equality → expect unsat
(declare-const available Int)
(declare-const reserved Int)
(declare-const quantity Int)
(declare-const db_available_written Int)
(declare-const db_reserved_written Int)
(declare-const logged_available Int)
(declare-const logged_reserved Int)
; Transition: line 33 — db.setAvailable commits (available + quantity)
(assert (= db_available_written (+ available quantity)))
; Transition: line 34 — db.setReserved commits (reserved - quantity)
(assert (= db_reserved_written (- reserved quantity)))
; Transition: line 36 — log computes from same original bindings
(assert (= logged_available (+ available quantity)))
(assert (= logged_reserved (- reserved quantity)))
; Negate: can the log diverge from what was written?
(assert (or
  (not (= logged_available db_available_written))
  (not (= logged_reserved db_reserved_written))
))
(check-sat)
; unsat — the logged values are provably identical to the DB-written values.
; The log is an accurate mirror of the committed state (even if that state is invalid).

; PRINCIPLE: P7
; VIOLATION: reserved - quantity committed as negative, then logged as "Release complete"
; Model: reserved comes from DB (>= 0), quantity is caller-supplied (> 0, unchecked upper bound)
(declare-const available Int)
(declare-const reserved Int)
(declare-const quantity Int)
(declare-const db_reserved_written Int)
(declare-const logged_reserved Int)
; DB read at L30: reserved is non-negative (DB invariant, no contract violated yet)
(assert (>= reserved 0))
; L29: available is non-negative
(assert (>= available 0))
; quantity is caller-supplied — releaseStock performs no check: quantity <= reserved
(assert (> quantity 0))
; Transition L34: db.setReserved commits reserved - quantity
(assert (= db_reserved_written (- reserved quantity)))
; Transition L36: log reads same arithmetic — mirrors committed value
(assert (= logged_reserved (- reserved quantity)))
; No guard between L30 and L34 enforces quantity <= reserved
; Violation reachable: quantity > reserved
(assert (> quantity reserved))
; Consequence: negative value is committed AND logged as "Release complete"
(assert (< db_reserved_written 0))
(assert (= logged_reserved db_reserved_written))
(check-sat)
; sat — negative reserved stock is committed at L34 and confirmed at L36 as "complete"
; Witness: reserved=2, quantity=5 → db_reserved_written=-3, logged_reserved=-3

; PRINCIPLE: P3
; VIOLATION: negative quantity makes available decrease and reserved increase at line 36
; The log still announces "Release complete" despite inverse semantics
(declare-const available Int)
(declare-const reserved Int)
(declare-const quantity Int)
(declare-const logged_available Int)
(declare-const logged_reserved Int)
; DB read state: non-negative
(assert (>= available 0))
(assert (>= reserved 0))
; Transition L33: db.setAvailable(available + quantity) — quantity unchecked
(assert (= logged_available (+ available quantity)))
; Transition L34: db.setReserved(reserved - quantity)
(assert (= logged_reserved (- reserved quantity)))
; Caller supplies negative quantity — no guard in releaseStock prevents this
(assert (< quantity 0))
; Consequence: available DECREASES (opposite of release)
(assert (< logged_available available))
; Consequence: reserved INCREASES (opposite of release)
(assert (> logged_reserved reserved))
(check-sat)
; sat — negative quantity causes releaseStock to behave as reserveStock
; DB writes and log at L36 both confirm semantically inverted state as "Release complete"
; Witness: quantity=-3, available=5→2, reserved=4→7

; PRINCIPLE: P4
; VIOLATION: two releaseStock calls on the same productId
; First call mutates reserved; second call reads the reduced value and may overdraw
(declare-const reserved_0 Int)
(declare-const available_0 Int)
(declare-const quantity_1 Int)
(declare-const quantity_2 Int)
(declare-const reserved_after_call1 Int)
(declare-const available_after_call1 Int)
(declare-const logged_reserved_call2 Int)
; Initial DB state
(assert (>= reserved_0 0))
(assert (>= available_0 0))
; Both calls use positive quantities
(assert (> quantity_1 0))
(assert (> quantity_2 0))
; First call: transitions at L33-L34 mutate shared DB state
(assert (= reserved_after_call1 (- reserved_0 quantity_1)))
(assert (= available_after_call1 (+ available_0 quantity_1)))
; First call succeeds (quantity_1 <= reserved_0)
(assert (<= quantity_1 reserved_0))
; Second call L30 reads the mutated reserved state
; Second call L36: logs reserved_after_call1 - quantity_2
(assert (= logged_reserved_call2 (- reserved_after_call1 quantity_2)))
; Violation: second call overdraws what remains
(assert (> quantity_2 reserved_after_call1))
(assert (< logged_reserved_call2 0))
(check-sat)
; sat — two sequential releases on the same product drive reserved negative
; Witness: reserved_0=5, q1=3, reserved_after=2, q2=3 → logged=-1

; PRINCIPLE: P6
; VIOLATION: quantity = 0 passes all code paths and reaches line 36 unchallenged
; DB state is unchanged; log still announces "Release complete"
(declare-const available Int)
(declare-const reserved Int)
(declare-const quantity Int)
(declare-const logged_available Int)
(declare-const logged_reserved Int)
; DB read state
(assert (>= available 0))
(assert (>= reserved 0))
; Transition L33-L34: writes happen regardless of quantity value
(assert (= logged_available (+ available quantity)))
(assert (= logged_reserved (- reserved quantity)))
; Degenerate: zero quantity — no guard exists before L33
(assert (= quantity 0))
; Consequence: DB and log show identical pre/post state — a committed no-op
(assert (= logged_available available))
(assert (= logged_reserved reserved))
(check-sat)
; sat — a zero-quantity release is committed and logged as "Release complete" with zero effect
; This masks upstream logic errors (wrong productId, wrong quantity binding, etc.)
